name: Kayla Simmons
chief_complaint: I can't stop throwing up and I feel so weak I can barely stand. My stomach is killing me and everything smells weird.
onset_description: College student who has had Type 1 Diabetes since age 11. Came down with a stomach bug 2 days ago and was unable to keep food or fluids down. Ran out of her long-acting insulin 4 days ago and has only been taking her rapid-acting insulin sporadically. Roommate called 911 when she found patient confused and breathing rapidly on the dorm room floor.

nausea_and_vomiting.description: I've been throwing up nonstop for two days. I can't even keep water down. My stomach just won't stop churning.
nausea_and_vomiting.character: persistent nausea with frequent non-bloody emesis, initially food contents then bilious

abdominal_pain.description: My whole stomach hurts, it's crampy and constant. It's been getting worse all day.
abdominal_pain.location: diffuse abdomen, worse in epigastric region

rapid_breathing.description: I can't catch my breath. I keep having to take these big deep breaths. My roommate said I was breathing really fast and weird.
rapid_breathing.character: Kussmaul respirations â€” deep, rapid, labored breathing as metabolic compensation for acidosis

confusion_and_lethargy.description: Everything is foggy. I can't think straight. My roommate says I wasn't making sense when she found me.
confusion_and_lethargy.character: altered mental status ranging from confusion to drowsiness, GCS approximately 13

excessive_thirst_and_urination.description: Before the vomiting started I was drinking water constantly and peeing all the time. Like going to the bathroom every 30 minutes.
excessive_thirst_and_urination.onset: 4 days ago (correlating with running out of long-acting insulin)
excessive_thirst_and_urination.character: polydipsia and polyuria preceding the acute illness

weakness_and_fatigue.description: I have zero energy. My muscles feel like jelly. I could barely walk to the bathroom this morning.

labs.BMP: Na 128 (low), K 5.6 (high â€” critical), Cl 92 (low), CO2 8 (critically low), Cr 1.8 (elevated), BUN 34 (elevated), Glucose 486 mg/dL (critically elevated)
labs.ABG: pH 7.12 (severe acidosis), pCO2 18 (compensatory hyperventilation), pO2 108, HCO3 6 (critically low)
labs.CBC: WBC 14.2 (elevated, stress response common in DKA), Hgb 15.8 (hemoconcentrated), Plt 278
labs.urinalysis: Large ketones, large glucose, specific gravity 1.035 (concentrated)
labs.lipase: 42 U/L (normal, rules out pancreatitis as cause of abdominal pain)

past_medical_history: Previous DKA episode (age 15, during adolescent non-compliance)
History of disordered eating (insulin omission for weight management â€” 'diabulimia' concern)
medications: Insulin glargine 18 units at bedtime (RAN OUT 4 DAYS AGO)
Insulin lispro per carb counting ratio (1:10), has been using sporadically
allergies: Adhesive tape (contact dermatitis â€” relevant for insulin pump or CGM if used)

social_history.alcohol: Admits to drinking at college parties 1-2 times per week, sometimes binge drinking
social_history.living_situation: Lives in a dorm room with one roommate. Parents live 3 hours away. No car on campus.
social_history.exercise: Runs 3-4 times per week, has been losing weight recently
social_history.diet: Irregular eating schedule. Sometimes skips meals. Roommate reports she has been eating less and less. Has mentioned wanting to lose weight.

conditions.mother: Hashimoto's thyroiditis, anxiety disorder, alive at 46

personality: Scared and embarrassed. Knows she messed up by running out of insulin and not refilling it. Alternates between tearful and defensive. Downplays her non-compliance. May not be fully honest about insulin omission for weight control unless asked in a non-judgmental way.
communication_style: Speaks in short phrases when confused, more articulate as she improves. Uses casual young adult language. May deflect serious questions with humor or sarcasm when uncomfortable. Becomes emotional when parents are mentioned. Answers questions about diabetes management evasively.
pain_description_style: Describes nausea and abdominal pain more than anything else. Rates pain functionally ('I can't eat anything,' 'I can't stop puking'). Not focused on the pain number â€” more focused on how terrible she feels overall.

critical_findings: Severe DKA: glucose 486, pH 7.12, HCO3 6, anion gap 28, beta-hydroxybutyrate 8.2
Critical hyperkalemia (K+ 5.6) â€” risk of cardiac arrhythmia (will drop rapidly with insulin and fluid therapy)
Severe dehydration with hypotension (BP 92/56) and tachycardia (HR 128)
Altered mental status (GCS ~13) indicating severe DKA
Insulin non-compliance: ran out of long-acting insulin 4 days ago
Concern for diabulimia (insulin omission for weight management) â€” needs sensitive screening
HbA1c 12.1% indicating chronic poor glycemic control
Hypokalemia will develop rapidly once insulin therapy begins â€” must replace K+ aggressively
Low phosphorus and magnesium requiring monitoring and replacement
Alcohol and marijuana use may contribute to management challenges
differential_diagnoses: Hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state (HHS) â€” usually Type 2
Sepsis-triggered DKA (no clear infectious source here)
recommended_interventions: Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation: 0.9% NS 1-2L bolus, then 250-500 mL/hr
Continuous IV insulin infusion at 0.1 units/kg/hr (do NOT bolus)
Potassium replacement: add 20-40 mEq KCl per liter of IV fluids once K+ drops below 5.3 (it will drop rapidly with insulin)
Q2-4 hour BMP for electrolyte monitoring (especially potassium)
Transition to D5 0.45% NS when glucose reaches 200-250 mg/dL (continue insulin until gap closes)
Continuous cardiac monitoring for arrhythmias (hyperkalemia risk then hypokalemia risk)
Serial ABGs or venous blood gases until acidosis resolves
Transition to subcutaneous insulin only AFTER gap closes AND patient tolerates PO
Overlap IV and subQ insulin by 1-2 hours before stopping drip
Social work and psychology consult for diabetes management barriers, diabulimia screening
Ensure insulin prescription is accessible (pharmacy, cost, insurance)
